user_history_reviews.review_text:
A lot of people like wireless headphones because it's the latest and greatest, but those people obviously don't spend an extended amount of time with their headphones on. Personally, one of the reasons I enjoy having these wired headphones is sometimes wireless headphones run out of battery power, and therefore, you need wired headphones. Also the PS5 doesn't support most third-party bluetooth headsets to begin with, so having Sony's headphones allows you to be able to use them with the PS5 without any problems.

Comfort :
These headphones are very comfortable to wear which makes using them for longer periods easy and enjoyable. This may sound like a simple thing, but can make or break a headphone experience, especially for gaming.

Design :
I love the design of these headphones since they are by Sony and match the PS5 design and look great next to a white ps5 controller and console.

Features:
This headset includes a microphone for chatting and has a very simple way to turn it on or off by moving it up or down. When it is turned on or off a nice clicking sound is heard. There is also a nice volume wheel on the left side of the headphones which is also a nice feature to have. These also work on a PC and likely anything with a regular headphone jack, which is a great feature.

Sound :
The sound is able to be customized via an official inzone hub program or a PS5 app that works with audio equalization. This allows you to fine tune things like bass to your liking. I played around with this and added a little to the bass and was able to easily fine tune the sound to my liking. You can also raise the volume through this program, so if they aren't as loud as you would prefer you can adjust it.

One more thing you need to bear in mind, this is a $100 pair of headphones not the $400 OEM premium headphones so it isn't going to be exactly like those. With a little fine tuning you can have your Sony headphones sounding good for whatever games you enjoy, just like I did.

I love these headphones and very glad that I got them! I highly recommend them.

My OEM power head strap cracked, which really upset me. I'm actually glad now that it happened because this is twice as nice and much more comfortable. It has a solid build and the material is out of this world! It fits very nice, is secure, and is very balanced.
The 360 degree adjustable knobs make sizing it to your head very easy and effortless. The sponge pads are easy to clean and utilize PU leather and memory foam. The 5,200 mAh power pack can extend playtime to over 4 hours or 6 hours of watching movies. It is rechargeable with a USB C cable, and it is not very heavy when wearing it.
I wish my Oculus Quest 2 came with this originally, it would have made all the difference in the world. I highly recommend it!

Not only is this motor the exact motor that is in my Samsung dryer, but it is a fraction of the price. All the mounts and parts are commercial grade, and it comes with a decent warranty. It installs fairly quickly, and most people should be able to switch it out within a half hour.  I highly recommend.

I wasn't paying attention when I ordered these reusable pods that they were specifically for the Ninja dual brew coffee maker. I have a regular standard Keurig machine, which has a single spout that comes down directly into the center. These Ninja reusable pods have three holes, so as expected they did not work. So being the DIYer guy that I am, I used an 1/8 of an inch drill bit and just put a small hole in the center and now these work perfectly on my Keurig.
These pods are of good quality and are much nicer than the replacement pods that they are replacing. I highly recommend them, even for a Keurig if you're willing to drill!

I am not a professional but I love taking photos and tweaking them. I have used basic programs and a few professional grade programs so I have some basic experience editing photos. This program compares well with most of the big name brands out there. It is fairly easy to use and offers similar tools and results. It would be nice if this was cheaper or offered a huge improvement over the competition but realistically it performs about the same as others available. As a beginning/intermediate level photography tool this works well.

*** Positive Points: ***

- Easy and quick installation on Windows 10
- Loads quickly on my core i5 with dedicated GPU
- Has most (if not all) of the features that other big name brand program have)
- Easy to use and relatively low learning curve

*** Negative Points;***

- Price is on par with other photo software from big names (this is not really cheaper)
- Learning curve (while still being fairly easy to overcome) is still present

The lighting effects are very cool and bright without being distracting. The inside has a nice logo that lights up giving it a nice high end look. The only real downside is that there is no place to put the USB receiver when not in use. I normally put it inside the back cover. Overall for the money this is a bargain.

+++ Positive Points +++
- Ergonomic and feels good on the hand
- silent clicks and smooth performance
- light effects look good but are not distracting
- has a replaceable internal battery
- can be used with the USB C cable if needed

--- Negative Points: ---
- No place to keep the USB receiver when not in use

user_history_reviews.review_text:
After recently having eye surgery on both eyes, getting my lenses replaced, I know have perfect sight for once in my life. The slight down side to this is that after having surgery, my eyes are super sensitive to light in the short term. I can no longer wear my prescription shades so was looking for a stylish pair of shades that would both reduce the glare from the sun (I live in the South so there's a lot of sun) over the short term and also function over the longer term.

Looking around I came across these Bliz Fusion sunglasses. They are available in a selection of frame colors and tints. I went for the classic black frames with blue tint.

From the get go, the box itself is stylish. A good hefty black cardboard lined on the inside with a sort of suede to help stop getting the lenses scratched. Also included is another bottom fitting to the frames, in white and a spare nose piece, to give you option of having black upper frames with white lower. It may sound weird but this color combination actually works very well. The glasses also come in a very nice, almost silk feeling drawstring bag.

The glasses are quite sturdy with a straight leg. Very comfortable to wear with enough grip to keep them on your head whether your just strolling around or actually doing something physical like skiing or running. The lenses are 6cm tall with that wrap-around style. Not too big as to cover half your face but also not too small so that light leaks in.

Overall a very good and stylish pair of shades.

The Sims 3 Pets is a fantastic companion to Sims 3 that will leave fans of the game happy for a long time. I love how the pets are Sims themselves and have their own feelings, needs, etc. just like you would expect them to be. My point is that the programmers didn't just make them automatons like other objects such as car, they did it the right way.

There are tons of pets that you can use other than just the horses, dogs, and cats. There are lizards, turtles, birds, hamsters, etc. I got a shock/laugh when I asked my daughter where the bird was and she said "the cat ate it" and pointed to a pile of feathers on the ground.

I don't really have any issues with this other than a bug where EA released this without the pre-order Pet Store working. Since I expect this to be fixed soon and it's not the focal point of the game, I will let this slide.

This is a GREAT expansion pack that makes Sims 3 all that much better!!

**** HIGHLY RECOMMENDED

user_history_reviews.review_text: I have been using turbo tax every year for at least the last 10 years, except for the one year I gave H&R Block a try.  I ditched the  H&R Block and never used it again because it made it way to easy to miss deductions and to find out what you had missed.  There are a lot of things I love and hate about turbo tax, but mostly I like it.  The software interface and navigation is annoying, sometimes if I revisit a section it makes me answer the same questions again instead of remember that I had already answered all of that.  But what I like, is that I can dig down into the nitty gritty and see and compare literally everything about my taxes, as well as hop back and forth between sections if I want to (I tend to jump from topic to topic in the beginning to get the gist of whether I owe or not, then go back and revisit all the details of each topic).  The reports, comparisons, and online help are what really sets it apart. If there's a topic or dediction that I don't understand the rules of I can click help and immediately get a detailed explanationi of what it is.  So, as much as it has it's annoyances, I really can't imagine doing my taxes without it and will continue to purchase it year after year.

it's great to have the full featured pro Acrobat for creating PDF's, except for some stupid reason they decided to remove the option to flatten layers.  So if you are putting in a stamp on a document, without flattening it, anyone can lift your stamp and use it elsewhere.  I read on the internet that some have gotten around this by using a javascript to put that menu option back, but I was unable to get it to work and got too frustrated with the whole idea of having to add back a feature to such expensive software that i just quit using it.  And honestly, at this price, why it should be missing any essential features is beyond me.  I have a much cheaper Foxit PDF creator that does this just fine.  I'm also not a fan of the new menu layouts - its a huge learning curve compared to older versions and it seems like even after you hunt around and find the menu item you want, it requires multiple steps  to actually navigte to the menu item.  Overall the user interface feels very immature and unintuitive.

Love how easy it makes it to do my taxes, as well as being able to dig down into reports to see year by year comparisons. Hate that it always wants you to give them your personal info every time you open it so they can mail you marketing materials in the future. I mean really, I already bought their software, do they really need to send me more ads and stuff? I also wish they could find a way to streamline their code so that it would run a little quicker. I have a fairly new laptop with a ssd drive and it still runs pretty slow. But, since I haven't yet found a better alternative, I continue to use it year after year. I did try the H&R block tax cut one year, but it almost missed a key deduction and it was hard to go thru and compare to see what deduction was missing so I haven't used it again.
If you are unsure what version you need, you can always buy the basic version and updgrade from within the software if you find you need the deluxe or home and small business version.

This is definitely the best Angry Birds since the first one came out. The last 2 releases were really just clones of the original and didn't feature much new content at all.

Angry Birds Space changes the way the game is played. They now give you a line to see the route the bird will take, but the game definitely isn't easier as they've complicated matters with things like gravitational field entry and exit (including orbit).

The visuals, the music and the gameplay are all big plusses here that bring something fresh to the Angry Birds title.

I love a good thinking game.

This one starts off really easy. You simply click a blue block and it disappears - letting the red blocks on top of it fall away and disappear. The green ones stay safe. As the levels progress, it gets significantly harder, requiring more thought and better timing to get rid of all the red blocks and keep the green ones. It's just like red remover if you've played that, but looks a bit more graphically polished on my kindle fire.

If you like skill and thought based games - give this one a try. It's worth checking out.

I thought it was just going to be some lame timer app.

It's actually not- It lets you create custom workout plans based on whatever goals you might have. It does have a timer for between sets, but to me, the main usable feature here is the ability to create those goal based workout plans (It supports putting 10 different ones in - pushups, situps, squats, dips, pullups, and five generic ones). I also like the fact that it will record your progress over time (automatically) in the "diary" section. I want to know how much better I'm getting at something... That's what keeps me motivated to do it. The app works really well on the kindle fire too (the reminders actually pop up in the top too).

I would recommend this app for anybody who doesn't know how to create a workout plan but wants to.

<addition - the app doesn't mention how many days you should do the exercises because that's a preference you can set in the "configure" menu. if you can only work out two days a week, it will design your program for 2 days a week - the default is set to 3>

user_history_reviews.review_text:
Comfortable, lightweight, high quality, nice simple design. I'm not just impressed, I'm actually shocked. With so many gaming mice falling apart and having issues after just a couple months of moderate usage, a mouse lasting more than a year without double-clicking issues is unheard of nowadays. This is the best mouse I've used, no question, and I have tried dozens over the last couple of decades of using a PC. Truly glorious.

I also bought the wireless one (from their website directly) and it also works great so far.

The material is very soft and nice, but the straps slide around a lot during any kind of rigorous movement like dancing. Even if you plan on mostly laying or sitting down in VR, the belt will constantly slide up and down your waist. Tightening the straps does not help this problem very much, even if you make them as tight as possible, because the material is simply too soft and thin.

I've really tried my best to make these things work, but at the end of the day they're just going to lightly tug your headset upward which is just awkward and annoying. They end up restricting your movement more than anything else, and tripping over your cord sounds preferable to feeling like the mask is being yanked upward all the time. It's pretty much impossible to go prone, and even squatting or moving a little too far away from the anchor point is uncomfortable. Despite my negative review, you might still try them anyways, because with something as niche as VR it's worth trying any method you can. Just keep in mind a ceiling suspension can feel very restrictive and uncomfortable.

The scroll-wheel is cheap and low quality, and already feels inconsistent after a few days of light usage. Double-clicking after just a few months. The firmware is very annoying, and constantly changes your DPI. Logitech support is horrible, so don't expect to be able to easily RMA. Unless you want to try to solder it yourself, you're probably just throw it in the trash and buy something else.

For the low price I wasn't expecting much, yet I'm still disappointed. The only positive thing I can say is that the design is nice and simple, but it's clearly for very small hands. I strongly recommend you avoid Logitech in general, since I've had very bad experiences with several of their mice.

I normally wear medium or large, I got 2XL (XXL) for the oversized look. It's comfortable, soft, nice and heavy, and has a really nice hood. The colors are nice and vibrant. The sleeves are WAY smaller than I expected, and I think it's too expensive, but overall I'm happy with the purchase.

If the creators made a version of this hoodie with extra-long sleeves, I'd buy it without hesitation.

The fabric is scratchy to the touch, and has terrible build quality. I normally wear medium or large, and I got XXL for the oversized look - and yet it was somehow still too small. The fabric is so thin and flimsy it's practically transparent. Even if this hoodie was free, I doubt anyone would want to wear it, not even as a lightweight warm weather hoodie. There are better options out there, don't waste your time with this one.

My son has come a long way from his initial sheepish attempts at aviation on his phone to having a full set-up where he can really have an immersive experience. We love his interest in aviation and encourage his passion as he finishes school so we were thrilled to present this to him as an early Christmas gift.

Using Flight Simulator on a huge gaming monitor and/or his XBox really helps him develop his aviation faculties and at a fraction of the cost of flight training as he's still in university. LOL It is fairly simple to set up and connect the rudders/output source and is ready to get to work right away and you can also make simple adjustments to get it exactly to your optimal comfort. Response is precise with no glitching, mechanics are impressive and I can't recommend it enough for someone who loves aviation either as a hobby or wistful opportunity long gone or a budding captain of the sky. This is VERY fun and affordable and our wallets thank us!

AGU3LPNXOJ3LUHUNJD5QAZ3U563A.review_text:
I'm rather surprised this worked on my system. My gaming PC is an MSI GL63-series laptop with a Geforce 1660 TI Mobile graphics card. Not exactly the Lambo of gaming laptops or graphics, but it handles well enough for every game I've put it up against.
All the 'DisplayPort needed' talk made me wiggle too, but an MDP-to-DP adapter make it work perfectly. (Heck, it came with one in the packaging, too)

Pros:
So far, games work just fine on mediumish quality with little to no lag issues. I play Half Life: Alyx on Medium and it works very well, Beat Sabre give no trouble, and VRchat also operated quite well. The room-tracking actually impressed me, it was quite near 1:1 movement with maybe 2-15 ms delay at worst and that was because of the laptop most likely. It presented an appropriate sense of scale within applications, as well as ample crouch detection, which you need for some games behind cover!

The button configuration works well enough for all apps since SteamVR is handling most of the input mappings to known VR systems I suppose. Sure, it doesn't have a hand strap like the Index... so I use the wrist straps and don't drop my controllers. I don't hand per-finger tracking, but I got grips, and the interfaces are fine with that. Sure, I popped extra for the room sensors, it made logical sense so my clumsy self won't bump into the TV or couch.

Cons:
The cable can bunch up a bit after a few dozen turns around the room, I'll attest to that. Sort of wish the earphones would better press to my ears, but I can hear through them fine while they hover just a half-inch away from my ears. Wouldn't've minded a 3.5mm plug to auxiliary headphones. Mic works in VRchat clearly however.
Maybe this is just me still finding my VR legs, but after a few hours of usage, I do notice a 'tactile disparity' with the real world the next day. It's gotten better, probably need to tune that side eye-spacing knob a little better. But that knob ain't great and I can't go below 60mm which might help some smaller folk.
Sure I had to make a Vive account during setup but... at least it ain't hocking my personal information or locking my games to a Meta account like some VR solutions might be doing. I don't HAVE to use the Vive storefront; SteamVR interfaces with it just fine. In fact, it's what does the actual room setup.

Summary:
I mean, sure the Index would be baller, but some of us have more important things like... you know... rent or groceries or bills. Do you NEED a $1,000+ VR setup? ... I mean REALLY NEED it? Do you need a $1,000+ phone? I mean REALLY NEED it? Do you NEED a car with power windows or can you just use the hand crank to achieve the same outcome and save a couple hundred bucks?

Moderation in all things; the middle option is oftentimes sufficient. The HTC Cosmos Elite is plenty sufficient for its intended use case scenarios.

AEB5R4AU3242FXZFI6LJWQMYHKXA.review_text:
This is the new Oculus Quest 2, which is basically exactly the same as the older Quest except that it comes with half the memory, 128Gb of inbuilt storage rather than 256Gb and is priced $100 cheaper than the 256Gb version.

The Quest 2 is the perfect entry level headset to find out whether or not you like the VR world. Some people don't like VR for various reasons including vertigo and the feeling of getting sick, neck pain as the headsets are fairly heavy on your head etc. Other people love it and spend hours on it.

For your money your will get the headset itself, a fairly comfortable elastic strap to attach it to your head, two controllers and a charging cable. Setup is fairly simple. You connect it to your phone using bluetooth and just follow the prompts. You will need a Facebook account to tie it to and this puts some people off (I understand why) but the majority of people already have a facebook account anyway.

The Quest 2 has 2 1832x1920 LCD screens. Not quite 4K but a higher resolution than HD. For the money this isn't bad, especially considering the higher resolution Rift S is double the price. The eye pieces are slightly adjustable to 3 different positions, dependent upon the size of your head and space between your eyes. One great thing about the LCD display is that you can adjust the frame rate. I have mine set to 72Hz which I feel is much smoother than default 60Hz setting and this makes me much less prone to feeling sick. There is also a 90Hz refresh rate however for PC gaming that is too high unless you have a seriously beefy graphics card. Note however that you do not need a PC to use this.

The headset also has 3D capable audio built directly into the headset. The sound quality is OK but nothing outstanding. As such a lot of people also wear headphones when using the Quest 2 and there is a built in 3.5mm audio port to hook your headphones into. Personally, the sound is good enough for me and I prefer using it without the added weight of a pair of headphones.

The headset is quite heavy and although the soft elastic strap that comes with it is adequate, I would highly recommend purchasing one of the Elite, hard backed straps that you can find on amazon. These do make a lot of difference in distributing the weight, making wearing the headset much more comfortable and you can wear it for longer.

The Quest 2 has hundreds of apps and games available for it, from watching netflix and shooting games to fitness and boxing games. Even games such as boxing do help you to get in shape as you quickly find yourself ducking and weaving as well as throwing punches as you get immersed in the gameplay. These games are stored in the 128Gb of memory in the headset and aren't that large so even with 128Gb you should have plenty of room for a number of games and apps.

You can also attach your headset to your PC and play VR games on your PC. To do this you will need to buy an Oculus cable. The official one comes in at $80 which is very pricey. You can find third party cables that do the exact same thing for much less. I love to play DCS and Flight Simulator 2020 on my PC with the Quest. The immersion takes it to a whole other level, however in order to do so you will also need a fairly beefy PC to handle the graphics. My PC is a couple of years old, so its not powerful enough to turn up all the textures in Flight Sim to Ultra, but it is good enough to play with reasonably high definition. Honestly this is the main use I have for the Quest 2. At somepoint I will probably upgrade to the Rift S, but a new PC is in order before that.

So if your interested in dabbling with VR or don't have the budget to buy the Rift S then I would highly recommend the Quest 2. This 128Gb version, coming in at just $300 is well worth the money and I very highly recommend it.

AGDULJ3O7D3R4H6Y3NPZ3IWWGIQA.review_text:
I think the key to losing weight when it relates to exercise is to find something that keeps you interested and coming back for more. This is an answer that is different for everyone. I am not seriously overweight, but I could lose 15-20 lbs (I have been around 206 lbs for the past year+ and should be 185-190 lbs). I am not a couch potato, I just haven't found the right thing to keep me interested long enough to get myself to exercise day in and day out. When I heard about the new game by EA, I was instantly interested, being an NFL fan for nearly 20 years. I thought the idea was fantastic, and was quick to go out and buy NFL Training Camp, even at the $100 price point which is still a head scratcher to me.

Now this review is very very fresh, as I have only had the game for a very short time, and it was only released less than a week ago. However, right off the bat, I can see some of the pros and cons that jump out at me immediately:

PROS

- More than anything else, I love the concept. This will garner a lot of interest from males, and while its the same types of exercises found in EA Active, this is tailored to a non-female audience.

- There is a wide variety of workouts available, exercises, graphs, stats, etc. This game will keep you interested, as it's not the same old stuff day after day.

- The heart monitor is really, really cool. The straps that go around your leg and arm are made well, and comfortable. I was very impressed by the quality of the hardware.

CONS

- I was very upset to see that in the very first workout that I did, the triceps exercise didn't work correctly. I did things exactly as the game instructed me to, and it just wouldn't work. I had to cheat and do something else to get past these exercises. I don't know how EA would fix this bug. Can they patch this online? For the price point of this game, there better be bug fixes that can be downloaded and applied or I will NOT be happy.

- The package didn't seem to come with instructions on how to tie the resistance band. This isn't a huge matter, but when the game loaded, it should have first instructed me how to correctly tie these. I also feel that the band might break at some point. Probably not, but some more information for the first time user what they can and cannot do with the band should have popped up in a video to start.

All in all, I love the idea, but I am very concerned about future problems I am going to have with the game understanding what exercises I am doing. If EA can provide patches that can be applied online, my review goes up to 5 stars. If they do not, I will be dropping this down to 2-3 stars and contacting EA support. For $100 I expect things to work flawlessly, I don't think that's too much to ask.

Look for this review to be updated in the future, hopefully I can get in the habit of using this nearly every day.

**** RECOMMENDED (but holding out my final opinion until I get a better sense from EA how seriously they will be addressing issues)

-----------------

UPDATE 11/26/2010

The initial problem that I was having with the tricep exercises was due to the heart rate monitor being upside down. Shouldn't the strap know if it isn't in the right position?

So that issues is fixed, but today pushups wouldn't register correctly.

However, I am really enjoying the 60 day challenge thus far, it gives a great sweat and is fun :)

-----------------

UPDATE 11/29/2010

Still loving this WII 'game' nearly a week later and am up to Workout #3 of the 60 Day Challenge. Already lost a pound but I'll really start believing if my scale gets under 200 lbs. which I haven't seen in years.

I'm bumping this up to ***** and making this a big recommendation. I still think it's a little pricey, but it is what it is.

BTW 1 person suggested ditching the resistance band and just getting 10-15 lb. free weights. I think this is a great idea and don't see any reason why this couldn't be done.

***** HIGHLY RECOMMENDED

-----------------

UPDATE 12/07/2010

Still using this 2 weeks later and it's getting harder, even with the 60 day program on "easy". One complaint that I have is that the game doesn't alert you for routines when you need to keep the wiimote in your right hand, so sometimes you are yelling at the tv when something isn't working. The programmers should have been able to note this as well if you didn't have the remote in your hand for the exercises you need to hold it. Also, there are times when you are tired at the end and don't want to have to look at the tv to figure out when to end the stretch hold, and the commentator doesn't tell you to switch sides at the midway point. Somewhat minor issues, but worth noting. These don't change my recommendation on the product though.

-----------------

UPDATE 12/20/2010

Almost a month into the 60 day challenge and I think it's the mark of a great routine when you still exercising nearly a month later, the workouts don't feel too long, and you are still going strong. I'm not losing weight yet but I don't know if that's because I really haven't changed my diet or have gained some muscle, but my muscles are sore much of the time, sore in a good way. I have had knee trouble for years and I have to say that with all the squats they have you do (and it's a TON) it can hurt quite a bit, but I don't quit, and am still fired to keep going!! I figure I'll eventually see the pounds start to come off, and if I ever get below 200 lbs (I am only at 203 right now at 6'1" so not very overweight at all) I will probably be a believer for life!

Main main frustration is with the DB squat forward and back routines. These are very difficult to do correctly and I find myself getting very aggravated doing these drills (but I still do them).

The structure of the workouts as a whole is sooo good. Warmups get you lose, cooldowns do what they are supposed to do, some drills hurt like hell, some tire you out a ton (WR sprints come to mind), some are easier, some are just plain fun (QB stuff). All in all, it's a fantastic balance!!

-----------------

UPDATE 12/28/2010

Major server issue this weekend with EA. Not only can thousands of people not get online, the data has been corrupted due to a bug with this issue. If this is not corrected very soon, I will be dropping my review 1 or 2 stars. At this price, with a company this big, this is completely unacceptable.

I had problems with the monitors tonight where I needed to change the battery mid-workout and had no idea what was going on. Would be nice if the game could keep track of battery charge as well. Doesn't seem that difficult since the wiimotes are able to do it.

Nearing 40 days of the 60 day challenge. Lots and lots and lots of squats (muy ugg) and I am not losing any weight, but I think the weight is being replaced with muscle instead. Still going strong!!

-----------------

UPDATE 01/06/2011

Up to 40+ days of the 60 day challenge and man it's getting harder. One of the workouts I did the previous week was my first 300 calorie day and 45 minutes long. I couldn't imagine doing medium or hard for this and don't plan to when I do my 2nd 60 day challenge starting next month.

Not sure if this is relevant or not, but I am a 3x open heart surgery survivor with an artificial heart valve, so I am a great candidate to show that this is a safe, healthy cardio option for many, many people out there!! I take atenolol for my heart so my heart rate is far less than everyone else in my online group which is kind of funny.

I wish that EA had built in support for dumbbells instead of assuming all users would be using the resistance band as there are workouts when I have to hold the wiimote in my right hand with the dumbbell at the same time. I don't know how they could have gotten around this without a 2nd sensor, but the dumbbell option is the way everyone should go. It's a better workout and more challenging.

Still no weight loss but there is no doubt I am gaining more muscle in my legs and arms. I've been drinking more water but haven't changed my diet much else, so the weight loss might take a little longer until I decide to get more serious about this aspect of my workouts.

-----------------

UPDATE 01/11/2011

OK I finally have some real complaints about this application, and having juuust finished my workout (the first of phase 3), I am going to bullet list these issues:

- My exercise routine tonight was 24 sets, and of the 24, 8 were warm-up/cool-down. That leaves 16 exercises, 8 of which were jump squats or bag hops, some 2 in a row! This is totally unrealistic. I have the setting on easy for the 60 day challenge, and I am only moderately overweight (204lbs, I should be around 180-185) with knees that give me trouble, 3 open heart surgeries and an artificial heart valve. The problem is that people who are 350 lbs are not going to be able to do jump squats and bag hops over and over and over again. There needs to be some setting to determine what kind of 60 day challenge you want --- 1 tailored towards DBs which is what it seems like the default is, or 1 for quarterbacks and lineman. I don't mind doing these routines, but when I have to start taking major breaks and having bad form, this defeats the purpose. This isn't medium or hard, it's E-A-S-Y and I find the heavy, heavy, HEAVY quad and squat work to be way overbalanced away from strength and just conditioning. Balance is totally off and it's starting to sour me, which is an unfortunate for a piece of software I think is really good.

- There needs to be a setting for what type of coach you want to have barking in your ear. Kind of like a Bill Parcells -> Peter Carroll meter. Sometimes when I am working my tail off and the game is telling me to move it is really not encouraging. I don't mind getting pushed, but the application should be able to tell when you are having trouble and curtail the abuse a bit. Like the last point, this is starting to sour me.

- There has to be a clearer explanation of what the software wants you to do with your stances for these sets. Sometimes we are supposed to mirror our virtual workout partner, sometimes we aren't. A more clear cue on the screen needs to remind you what side you should expect to start on.

- Relating to that note, I am so sick of not having an option to not hold the remote on some of the sets where I use dumbbells instead. Sometimes the wiimote is barely in my fingers, as I have to clutch the dumbbell first and foremost.

I am starting to get concerned how seriously EA is taking this software. They have already slashed the price in half after I paid $100 for this in November (thanks EA), and are ignoring my forum posts and support emails about the stats corruption issue that occurred December 27-28th. Hopefully this is just growing pains, as I am still motivated to use Training Camp. I have missed 5 days in my routine due to scheduling conflicts, but I plan to really kill it these next couple of days to get back on track.

I'm glad people are enjoying my review and ongoing blog. It seems to be popular, thanks for following my progress.

-----------------

UPDATE 01/12/2011

Related to what I was saying yesterday, I am sick and tired of these workouts where they have you do the same thing over and over again in unrealistic amounts relative to the challenge level you have selected. Yesterday it was bag hops and squat jumps, this time it was 3 sets of prone knee drives/mountain climbers, with 45 reps per set. My form was terrible in the 2nd set and by the 3rd I just cancelled the set as I couldn't do it. More QA with balance of the workouts especially at easy needed to be done, as this is completely unrealistic. I ended up doing 91% of the workout but had to cancel out of 2 sets to be able to survive. I also turned off coaching comments completely as I couldn't take being yelled at while I was working my tail off.

Still a few days behind but I was too sore to do a double yesterday. This 60 day challenge might end up being 65 or so by the time I am done, we shall see.

-----------------

UPDATE 01/19/2011

I came down with the flu end of last week so I haven't been able to work out in 5-6 days. 60 day challenge is going to be pushed back because of this.

One big negative is that EA Support said there was nothing they could do about the bad data on the servers so that is a huge negative in my eyes. If the #s aren't accurate, why trust them? NOT. GOOD. EA.

-----------------

UPDATE 02/16/2011

Flu lasted longer than I thought and I ran into some home repair issues (water damage) with storms up in New England so I haven't touched NFL Training Camp in weeks. I also was upset at how EA support brushed me off and ignored my concerns about bad stats and also how after spending $100 in November they slashed the price in half 2 months later. So, I will be back using the program, but I need some time to recharge and take care of other things right now.

-----------------

UPDATE 03/03/2011

Now it's $40?!? I feel robbed EA. I pay $100 end of November, and 3 months later it's at bargain basement prices? Not cool at all, and it's made me incredibly unmotivated to use this, as I feel I have been scammed.

-----------------

UPDATE 05/15/2011

Back to NFL Training Camp but trying something different. I am making pre-defined workouts highlighting only the workouts I like. I hope this will keep me coming back for more. I'm still pissed about the price drop but it is what it is. I found the workout getting too difficult and not giving me much flexibility made me not want to play as often. I realize this loses the randomness and targeting areas I might not want to, but the most important thing is for me to be WANTING to do the workouts. We will see how this new plan works out...

-----------------

UPDATE 10/29/2011

I stopped using NFL Training Camp and joined a gym back in September. I was disgusted with how quickly EA slashed the price after I paid $100 and I got sick of how flaky it was at times. It's fun to use once in a while, but the lack of support from EA really soured it all for me. Final analysis is Not Recommended to to EA's lack of commitment to the product.

AGKLTVE3GM5ZP3K6J25JVDTOCBJA.review_text:
I purchased this game because I like the Xcom games and turn based games in general. I didn't realize that this was going to be a turn based game until the reviews started being released. I can understand why people are complaining. It's not what I expected when I saw the ads for this game. I have only cursory knowledge of the Marvel universe. I haven't read the comics or seen the movies. The extent of my Marvel knowledge from Wanda Vision.

I am really enjoying this game though, despite various quirks. The turns are card based and you can upgrade and modify the cards for each character. It took a few challenges before I adjusted to a card based game and now I am finding that I like it. You get to use 3 cards per turn, plus earn points to use environmental damage. Each character has unique skills and you can match characters together with cards that complement each other. I have been playing on normal mode and have found the game to be much easier than Xcom (which also makes it a lot more fun to play).

Another fun aspect of the game is exploring the grounds of your base. It's been fun uncovering mysteries and collecting various objects. I especially like finding paintings to hang on the walls of the abbey and to upgrade various things in the abbey.

I've also enjoyed interacting with the different characters. Some of them have interesting stories and it's been fun getting to know them. That being said, there is way too much time spent on dialogue that doesn't move the story or build the characters. The voice acting is also very stilted, which makes connecting with the characters less engaging than other games. The graphics are below par too. The characters' facial expressions are muted because of the graphics simply aren't good enough to handle complex facial expressions. The aspect I find most annoying is watching the same little animated scenes over and over (such as when Tony crafts new cards), but i can live with it because I like the novelty of the other gameplay aspects.

Note, when this game was first released, it crashed a lot, but the game has since been patched and it hasn't crashed since the patch on my PS5.

Despite this game's flaws, I am still giving it 5 stars because it's very unique. I enjoy the card based mechanic. Building friendships is also fun and think this game is going in the right direction with friendship building, but it misses the mark. Overall, there are a lot of interesting ideas in this game, but it's not an A+ title. It's not at all what I would have expected from a super hero game though and for me that's a good thing.

AGKLTVE3GM5ZP3K6J25JVDTOCBJA.product_description: Sonic Frontiers - Nintendo SwitchWorlds are colliding in Sonic the Hedgehog's newest high-speed adventure! Accelerate to new heights, battle hordes of powerful enemies, and experience the thrill of high-velocity, open-zone acrobatic action and platforming freedom across the five massive Starfall Islands. Welcome to the evolution of Sonic games!Mario + Rabbids Sparks of Hope – Standard EditionTeam up with Mario, Luigi, Princess Peach, Rabbid Peach, Rabbid Luigi, and their friends on a galactic journey to defeat a malevolent entity and save your Spark companions. Explore planets throughout the galaxy as you uncover mysterious secrets and compelling quests!
AGKLTVE3GM5ZP3K6J25JVDTOCBJA.review_text:
I was expecting this game to be more challenging, but I have been able to beat all of the levels on the first try on the most difficult setting. For perspective, I found the first Mario + Rabbids challenging and had to play Wasteland 3 and X-Com on the easiest modes. I am by no means an expert in this type of game.

I had to plan out my moves and figure out which character would be the best match for each battle in the first Mario + Rabbids game. This game has none of that. Any of the characters can be used successfully in any of the levels. You get little helper sparks that each have special powers can be be swapped out between the characters. Without using the sparks, this game becomes a little more challenging, but I feel like the game should be more challenging without me having to remove a core game function.

This game can be quite frustrating in the parts where you're just walking around. Walking around the levels, you come across random enemies that are a much lower level than you. You have to wait through load screens to either flee or play if you're unfortunate enough to not avoid them. Exploration is less fun because of this. Why not let users simply dash the enemies that are a much lower level or why not have the enemies level up with the player? Exploration is also less fun because of there are a ridiculous number of load screens. For instance, you can explore different houses and normally, I like to go into different buildings in games and look around, but it's a miserable experience with this game because it takes so long to load.

I still found myself playing this game and enjoying some of the more challenging fights, but the moderately challenging parts were few and far between. There is nothing inherently broken in this game. I'm just not sure who the target audience is for this game. I think most kids would find slogging through the overworlds boring and the fights might be too challenging. For me, it just wasn't challenging enough, despite the fact that I'm not that good at this type of game and I can't remember a time when I didn't find a game challenging enough.

AFAZE65KD35TB64QV5STNQ4U6R5A.product_description: Enter mankind's greatest conflict with Battlefield V as the series goes back to its roots in a never-before-seen portrayal of World War 2. AFAZE65KD35TB64QV5STNQ4U6R5A.review_text:
I bought this from Walmart in case people wonder why I'm not a verified purchase. Anyway, there is some good and bad, but, overall, it's a world of improvement over bf1, which I didn't like very much.

Pros:
No DLC purchases or other stupid stuff. Shooting mechanics are good and it's an overall fun experience. The new points system effectively encourages squad play. Visuals are even better than bf1. The class system is back, so you can play as a medic or an assault, or a sniper or support. This all makes for a very fun game.

Cons:
Lots of places to hide, it's stupid easy to camp right now, I saw a video from Levelcap on youtube talking about one map that was way to dark, and they were eventually going to mess with the lighting to make it more difficult to camp, that would be a very good thing. The maps are a lot like bf1, in that in most areas of them, close range weapons are practically useless, I have yet to see a single person shoot at me with a shotgun.
More maps are coming, though, for free. There are also plenty of updates that are going to happen in the next couple years this is Dice's big game. So don't let those things discourage you.

Overall, this is an excellent game I'm happy I didn't wait to buy for $30

AGADSWBOE6IJXQKDYCFVVN7YBA2A.review_text:
I was skeptical in buying this game with all the negative reviews and low ratings. However I got it on a black Friday deal so I figured why not. I have played it for over a week now.

First off, I don't really know what people are crying about. The game is not that bad. in fact it is pretty fun. And I find it a lot like previous versions of fallout with minor differences
THINGS I HAVE NOTICED
1. The graphics look good. I play on a 65inch LG on an Xbox one S and I think the graphics are great. The open world looks awesome.
2. As many have griped about, there aren't any real NPCs in the game said from robots but who really cares.
3. You CAN attack other players once you reach level 5. However, you will get a bounty if that bothers you. Thankfully most of the community that I have come in contact with are pretty cool and don't mess with you too much.
4. when you die from another character you lose CAPS but not your gear which is cool
5. When you die from something else like a horde of super mutants that jump you (that happened), You loose your junk but not weapons armor and you have an opportunity to go retrieve it.
6. The open world is HUGE!!!! with a lot to explore. They have incorporated a C.A.M.P system which allows you to build a base wherever and as large as you want. However there are pros and cons to this
7. Despite what some individuals have complained about there are a lot of things in the game to do and lots that can kill you. but you are not bound to do anything but explore if you want

If you expecting to quickly unlock the best weapons and gear and go kill a lot of stuff then this game isn't for you.
As most have put it is more of a survival game. You must scavenge and craft (loot is plentiful) and modify weapons. You have to watch your food and water, health and Rads. There is a lot of missions and events but nothing as involved as previous versions of fallout. They still however, are entertaining.
ANNOYANCES
1. There are some minor glitches in the game like sometimes you may have to repeat pat of a quest that you already did.
2. The CAMP system can be cumbersome to use, and the method for saving your blueprints are kind of odd.
3. Collecting wood is annoying especially when I have a hatchet that I apparently cannot use on trees...really LOL
4. You seem to get hungry and thirsty ALOT which effects your AP.
Overall, I feel the game is worth buying, I don't know if I would of spent 65 bucks on it but it is definitely worth what I paid for it . Bethesda is actively patching and adding to the game so expect some large updates but this is a good thing --so be patient :) I did not give it 5 stars because it does have room for improvement but it is definitely not as bad as people make it out to be.

AGCSHQRXX6N4TA2ZNC5BZHQ6PQYA.product_description: Return to the ultimate medieval Battlefield! Chivalry 2 is a multiplayer first-person slasher inspired by epic medieval movie battles. Players are thrust into the action of every iconic moment of the era - from clashing swords, to storms of flaming arrows, sprawling castle sieges, and more. Dominate massive battlefields. Catapults tear the Earth apart as you lay siege to castles, set fire to villages, and slaughter filthy peasants in the return of team objective maps. Experience the next era of melee combat: Master the Blade with an all-new combat flow that combines our real-time strikes and free-flowing Combo system to speed up the action, and let players become a whirlwind of steel on the battlefield. Created with the ability to fight multiple foes in mind, Chivalry 2's combat mixes the best of both FPS and fighting game genres for an unmatched melee experience. Fully Forged for PlayStation 5 - Made to take full advantage of the PS5 hardware, Chivalry 2’s 64-player medieval madness runs in a glorious 4K at 60 FPS with beautifully rendered details,  ray-tracing, immersive haptic feedback, and other significant improvements made possible on PlayStation 5. Chivalry 2 also supports cross-generational upgrades from current gen versions when you upgrade to a PlayStation 5 system.
AGCSHQRXX6N4TA2ZNC5BZHQ6PQYA.review_text:
One of the best games that I’ve played in quite sometime. Pure chaos, 32v32 online medieval warfare. Lots of different classes and weapons to choose from as well.
The world is your battlefield.
Want to throw a chicken at someone? You can. Want to give someone splinters and smack them with a tree log? You can. Want to cut someone’s head off then smack their buddy with it? You can. Very diverse and wild gameplay.
At the moment it is kinda barebones, however. No stat section, theres crossplay but you can’t party up with crossplay friends(incredibly stupid), and while the maps are pretty incredible and diverse there’s only a handful right now. Most of these issues are coming soon, but who knows when that will be.

Great, addictive game otherwise.

AGCSHQRXX6N4TA2ZNC5BZHQ6PQYA.product_description: The Outriders: Worldslayer features 4 unique classes and a customizable arsenal of brutal powers, skills, gear and weapons you are unstoppable…but even the formidable eventually meet something to fear in this brutal 1-3 player co-op looter shooter. OUTRIDERS WORLDSLAYER is a brutal 1-3 player co-op looter shooter set in an original, dark sci-fi universe. The full experience of OUTRIDERS WORLDSLAYER includes the original OUTRIDERS game, with all the numerous quality of life improvements and Expedition content added since launch. You’ll create your own Outrider from one of 4 powerful classes and journey across the diverse and deadly planet Enoch. Begin with the original OUTRIDERS campaign or use the boost to jump straight into the Worldslayer campaign with a fully geared up level 30 Outrider. Here you will face off against the most deadly Altered ever encountered - Ereshkigal, in humanity’s last fight for survival. Beyond her even greater horrors exist in the ultimate endgame experience taking place in the ancient ruins of Tarya Gratar. Combining aggressive gunplay with violent powers and an arsenal of increasingly twisted customizable weaponry and gear-sets, OUTRIDERS WORLDSLAYER offers countless hours of visceral gameplay from one of the finest shooter developers in the industry – People Can Fly.
AGCSHQRXX6N4TA2ZNC5BZHQ6PQYA.review_text:
I’ve been waiting for this game for a while, and the end result is just an average shooter.
I’ve put about 5 hours in so far, and nothing has blown me away or come off as a new addition to the genre.
Graphics are below average, and the colors are extremely muddy, even on the seriesX.
Gameplay is clunky and not very smooth.
Story is forgettable and generic.
Most annoying issues I have
#1, server issues. I’m sure it’ll get fixed eventually, but the fact you need a constant connection to the server to play by yourself is absurd.
#2, the enemies just shoot and shoot, reload and shoot more. There’s no strategy behind what they do.

Overall it’s a fine game, just don’t get your expectations up.

AHJ3S3V3XBCJZFF7EE4RALKBJPPQ.product_description: Battlefield 4 is the genre-defining action blockbuster made from moments that blur the line between game and glory. Fueled by the next-generation power and fidelity of Frostbite 3, Battlefield 4 provides a visceral, dramatic experience unlike any other. Only in Battlefield can you demolish the buildings shielding your enemy. Only in Battlefield will you lead an assault from the back of a gun boat. Battlefield grants you the freedom to do more and be more while playing to your strengths and carving your own path to victory. In addition to its hallmark multiplayer, Battlefield 4 features an intense, dramatic character-driven campaign that starts with the evacuation of American VIPs from Shanghai and follows your squad's struggle to find its way home. There is no comparison. Immerse yourself in the glorious chaos of all-out war, found only in Battlefield.
AHJ3S3V3XBCJZFF7EE4RALKBJPPQ.review_text:
My son is a computer geek and game player. He loves this stuff. Battlefield 4 was a Christmas gift. Since then, well, we haven't seen much of him. He's been locked up in his room playing this game.

According to him, you don't want the download game. Even with fiber optic broadband internet, this game would have taken ages to download. Moreover, with all the updates required to update the disc loaded game it still took quite awhile to become a playable game.

The have itself is excellent having had some time to cure the bus that plagued earlier generations of the game. The transitions, layers, sounds and movement are very good. The fun/wow factor is very high.

When a 17 year old computer dweeb says the game is fun, well folks, the game IS fun.

AHABUIZ7FQENNDSJUT6YBKEXX34Q.review_text:
The Quest2 is acceptable for its price. Although you will have to spend quite a bit extra for a link cable, a headstrap upgrade, some nice earbuds/headphones, and so on. The controllers are surprisingly good, but nowhere near as good as the Valve Index "Knuckles" Controllers. The Quest2 will not give you the ultimate experience, but it will get you into VRchat or Beat Saber on a budget.

Normally, I'd give this headset around a 4/5, but there's 1 little issue. You must have a Facebook account, and you must link your Quest2 to your smartphone. You have to hand over basically all of your personal information just to use the thing, and currently there's no method to completely avoid this process. It's a massive violation of privacy, and unacceptable for a piece of hardware to require from its users.

Yes, you can give false information, but you risk getting banned and unable to use your headset anymore. Yes, there are ways to deal with all of this, but you must be very tech-savvy and be willing to invest hours into setting things up - and even then, there's always the chance of a stealth update ruining your whole set-up.

So yes, it's a decent headset, but even if it was literally free I would be hesitant to recommend it to anyone because of the Facebook integration.
